gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (24/358)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (24/358)

parts.text: عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ الْأَصَمِّ

text: أَمِيرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) سُئِلَ عَنْ رَجُلٍ نَذَرَ وَ لَمْ يُسَمِّ شَيْئاً قَالَ إِنْ شَاءَ صَلَّى رَكْعَتَيْنِ وَ إِنْ شَاءَ صَامَ يَوْماً وَ إِنْ شَاءَ تَصَدَّقَ بِرَغِيفٍ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Muhammad Bin Al Hassan Bin Shamoun, from Abdullah Bin Abdul Rahman Al Asamma, from Misma’a Bin Abdul Malik,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, that Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup> was asked about a man who vowed and did not specify anything. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘If he so desires to, he can Pray two Cycles, and if he so desire to he can Fast for a day, and if he so desires to he can give charity with a loaf (of bread)’.
en.sarwar: 18. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Muhammad ibn al-Hassan Shammun from ‘ Abd Allah ibn ‘Abd al- Rahman al-Asamm from Misma‘ ibn ‘Abd al-Malik who has narrated the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that ’Amir al-Mu’minin, Ali, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, was asked about a man who makes a vow and commitment without specifying anything, has said, ‘If he wants he can perform two Rak‘at Salat (prayer) or fast for one day or give one loaf of bread for charity.’”
